How do I define a record for what a slat is in Motoi?

```motoi
(define lines
  (list
    "(event :ts 1 :kind \"card.opened\")"
    "(event :ts 2 :kind \"card.closed\")"
    "(event :ts 3 :kind \"mood.changed\" :from :happy :to :curious)"))

(map slat-read lines)
```

A slat is flat because it's line-oriented. One record per line, when you want to stream them.

  (let ([events (read-mood-stream)])
    (let loop ([current :calm] [remaining events])

```motoi
(define (mood-at t)
  (let ([events (read-mood-stream)])
    (let loop ([current :calm] [remaining events])
      (cond
        ((null? remaining) current)
        ((> (slat-key (car remaining) 'ts) t) current)
        (else (loop (slat-key (car remaining) 'to) (cdr remaining)))))))

(mood-at #inst "2026-07-11T14:30:00Z")
```

(define (session-audit path session-id)
  (with-slat-stream path
    (lambda (stream)
      (filter
        (lambda (record)
          (case (car record)
            ((session-begin session-end)
               (equal? (slat-key record 'id) session-id))
            ((session-action)
               (equal? (slat-key record 'session) session-id))
            (else #f)))
        stream))))

How do I define a record for a worked assembly — one specific poem as a record in Motoi?

```motoi
(record poem
  :name             symbol
  :form             slat-ref              ; -> poem-form
  :speaker          symbol                ; who's talking in the poem
  :beloved-object   (or slat-ref 'none)   ; what the poem is about
  :setup            string                ; brief summary
  :turn             (or string 'none)     ; the volta / kireji / third-line
  :closing          string                ; how it ends
  :sketch           slat-ref              ; the sketch in book-of-words
  :public-domain    boolean)              ; can we quote it?

(poem
  :name          'ozymandias
  :form          'sonnet
  :speaker       'traveler-quoted-by-poet
  :beloved-object 'ruined-statue
  :setup         "traveler describes a ruined statue in the desert;
                   the pedestal boasts of empire"
  :turn          "the shift from the boast to the sand around it —
                   the words say 'despair'; the world says 'gone'"
  :closing       "the vast sands stretch far away — deep time as
                   the poem's last image"
  :sketch        'words-poetry-07-sonnet-ozymandias
  :public-domain #t)
```

The last piece. Individual poems can be records too.
